summary:
Proper cementation ensures sufficient bond strength for retaining a restoration without needing additional bulky materials; while minor mechanical aids can help, they are not as effective or necessary compared to precise technique during placement. The focus should be on accurate cementing methods over using more metal material. To summarize Agent2’s reasoning into two concise sentences focusing only on critical clinical aspects related to your query about supplementary retention on an abutment tooth:

Proper cementation provides essential bond strength needed for good retention without requiring added bulkier metallic components. Minor mechanical enhancements may support but do so less effectively than optimal cementation practices which remain crucial. 

This summary captures core elements regarding preferred method versus potential alternatives directly pertinent to achieving reliable dental restorations through appropriate technical means instead of unnecessary additions. Let me know if you need any other adjustments!
